channel.title: Data Breach & Ransomware Aggregator
channel.description: Real-time data breach notifications from state registries, federal sources, and ransomware trackers

item.title: Heart South Cardiovascular Group
item.description:
Heart South Cardiovascular Group reported a state registry affecting 46666 records in Alabaster, AL.
📊 Records Affected: 46666 | 📍 Location: Alabaster, AL | 📰 Source: Maine AG | 🏷️ Type: State Registry

item.title: Not Toying Around: Hasbro Attack May Take 'Weeks' to Remediate
item.description:
The company's 8-K filing notes "unauthorized access" and that it has activated business continuity plans and taken some systems offline.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Geopolitics, AI, and Cybersecurity: Insights From RSAC 2026
item.description:
AI-driven threats, global leadership shifts, and the future of cybersecurity in a rapidly evolving landscape were among the discussions at RSAC 2026 Conference.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Claude Source Code Leak Highlights Big Supply Chain Missteps
item.description:
Or, why the software supply chain should be treated as critical infrastructure with guardrails built in at every layer.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Blast Radius of TeamPCP Attacks Expands Amid Hacker Infighting
item.description:
As organizations disclose breaches tied to TeamPCP's supply chain attacks, ShinyHunters and Lapsus$ are getting involved, taking credit, and creating a murky situation for enterprises.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: OWASP GenAI Security Project Gets Update, New Tools Matrix
item.description:
In recognition of 21 generative AI risks, the standards groups recommends that companies take separate but linked approaches to defending GenAI and agentic AI systems.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Automated Credential Harvesting Campaign Exploits React2Shell Flaw
item.description:
An emerging threat cluster tracked as UAT-10608 is exploiting vulnerable Web-exposed Next.js apps and using an automated tool to exfiltrate credentials, secrets, and other system data.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Axios Attack Shows Social Complex Engineering Is Industrialized
item.description:
The attack on the popular NPM package Axios is just one of many targeting maintainers and has shone a light on how threat actors can scale sophisticated social engineering campaigns.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI-Assisted Supply Chain Attack Targets GitHub
item.description:
PRT-scan is the second in recent months where a threat actor appears to have leveraged AI for automated targeting of a widespread GitHub misconfiguration.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Mobile Attack Surface Expands as Enterprises Lose Control
item.description:
Shadow AI embedded in everyday apps, combined with outdated mobile devices and zero-click exploits, is creating a new and largely unseen mobile risk. The post Mobile Attack Surface Expands as Enterprises Lose Control appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: In Other News: ChatGPT Data Leak, Android Rootkit, Water Facility Hit by Ransomware
item.description:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Symantec vulnerability, anti-ClickFix mechanism added to macOS, FBI hack classified as major incident. The post In Other News: ChatGPT Data Leak, Android Rootkit, Water Facility Hit by Ransomware appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Asian Government Attacks
item.description:
A Chinese threat actor exploited the video conferencing platform to perform reconnaissance, escalate privileges, and execute additional payloads. The post TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Asian Government Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: European Commission Confirms Data Breach Linked to Trivy Supply Chain Attack
item.description:
Hackers stole over 300GB of data from the Commission’s AWS environment, including personal information. The post European Commission Confirms Data Breach Linked to Trivy Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Fortinet Rushes Emergency Fixes for Exploited Zero-Day
item.description:
The improper access control bug in FortiClient EMS allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code remotely. The post Fortinet Rushes Emergency Fixes for Exploited Zero-Day appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: North Korean Hackers Target High-Profile Node.js Maintainers
item.description:
The threat actor behind the Axios supply chain attack has been aiming at other maintainers in its social engineering campaign. The post North Korean Hackers Target High-Profile Node.js Maintainers appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Google DeepMind Researchers Map Web Attacks Against AI Agents
item.description:
A vulnerability named ‘AI Agent Traps’ allows attackers to manipulate, deceive, and exploit visiting agents via malicious web content. The post Google DeepMind Researchers Map Web Attacks Against AI Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Wynn Resorts Says 21,000 Employees Affected by ShinyHunters Hack
item.description:
The high-end casino and hotel operator has likely paid a ransom to avoid a data leak. The post Wynn Resorts Says 21,000 Employees Affected by ShinyHunters Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: German police unmask two suspects linked to REvil ransomware gang
item.description:
The suspects were named as Daniil Shchukin, a 31-year-old Russian national believed to have used the alias UNKN (UNKNOWN), and Anatoly Kravchuk, a 43-year-old Ukraine-born Russian citizen who investigators say worked as a developer for the group.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Medusa ransomware group using zero-days to launch attacks within 24 hours of breach, Microsoft says
item.description:
Microsoft said it has been alarmed to see how effective Medusa actors are, citing multiple cases where the group can move from initial access to data exfiltration and ransomware deployment within 24 hours.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: FBI: Cyber fraud surges to $17.6 billion in losses as scams, crypto theft soar
item.description:
Cyber-enabled fraud was behind 85% of all losses reported to the FBI in 2025 and constituted 45% of the 1,008,597 complaints it's IC3 unit received overall.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
item.description:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
item.description:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
item.description:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
item.description:
The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
item.description:
A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
item.description:
An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Device code phishing attacks surge 37x as new kits spread online
item.description:
Device code phishing attacks that abuse the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant flow to hijack accounts have surged more than 37 times this year. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Axios npm hack used fake Teams error fix to hijack maintainer account
item.description:
The maintainers of the popular Axios HTTP client have published a detailed post-mortem describing how one of its developers was targeted by a social engineering campaign believed to have been conducted by North Korean threat actors. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Hackers exploit React2Shell in automated credential theft campaign
item.description:
Hackers are running a large-scale campaign to steal credentials in an automated way after exploiting React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182) in vulnerable Next.js apps. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: New FortiClient EMS flaw exploited in attacks, emergency patch released
item.description:
Fortinet has released an emergency weekend security update for a new critical FortiClient Enterprise Management Server (EMS) vulnerability that is actively exploited in attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Why Simple Breach Monitoring is No Longer Enough
item.description:
Infostealers are harvesting credentials and session cookies at scale, bypassing traditional defenses. Lunar explains why simple breach monitoring alone can't keep up with modern credential-based attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Microsoft links Medusa ransomware affiliate to zero-day attacks
item.description:
Microsoft says that Storm-1175, a China-based financially motivated cybercriminal group known for deploying Medusa ransomware payloads, has been deploying n-day and zero-day exploits in high-velocity attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Disgruntled researcher leaks “BlueHammer” Windows zero-day exploit
item.description:
Exploit code has been released for an unpatched Windows privilege escalation flaw reported privately to Microsoft, allowing attackers to gain SYSTEM or elevated administrator permissions. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: New GPUBreach attack enables system takeover via GPU rowhammer
item.description:
A new attack, dubbed GPUBreach, can induce Rowhammer bit-flips on GPU GDDR6 memories to escalate privileges and lead to a full system compromise. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: German authorities identify REvil and GandCrab ransomware bosses
item.description:
The Federal Police in Germany (BKA) has identified two Russian nationals as the leaders of GandCrab and REvil ransomware operations between 2019 and 2021. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Unknown
item.description:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Data Breach Reported by Orthopedic Implant Manufacturer TriMed
item.description:
TriMed, a Santa Clarita, California-based manufacturer of upper and lower orthopedic implants, has announced a data security incident involving unauthorized […] The post Data Breach Reported by Orthopedic Implant Manufacturer TriMed appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Free HIPAA Compliance Risk Calculator
item.description:
HIPAA compliance is mandatory for organizations that qualify as HIPAA covered entities. But how compliant is your organization really? With our 5-minute free HIPAA Risk Check, you can quickly evaluate the compliance status of your organization and receive a report with actionable insights to immediately improve compliance with HIPAA. The post Free HIPAA Compliance Risk Calculator appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital Data Breach More Than 257,000 Individuals
item.description:
Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital (NMH), a 226-bed hospital in Nacogdoches, Texas, has recently announced a data security incident that was first […] The post Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital Data Breach More Than 257,000 Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Eye Physicians of Central Florida Data Breach Settlement
item.description:
Eye Physicians of Central Florida has agreed to settle a class action lawsuit stemming from a 2023 data breach that […] The post Eye Physicians of Central Florida Data Breach Settlement appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Iowa AG Sues Change Healthcare Over 2024 Ransomware Attack
item.description:
Iowa Attorney General Brenna Bird has filed a lawsuit against Change Healthcare, UnitedHealth Group, and Optum over the February 2024 […] The post Iowa AG Sues Change Healthcare Over 2024 Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Cardiovascular Consultants Pays $3.85M to Settle Data Breach Litigation
item.description:
Cardiovascular Consultants in Arizona has settled a class action lawsuit stemming from a 2023 data breach involving the protected health […] The post Cardiovascular Consultants Pays $3.85M to Settle Data Breach Litigation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Stryker Fully Operational After March Cyberattack
item.description:
Stryker has announced that it has recovered systems impacted by its March 11 cyberattack and is once again fully operational […] The post Stryker Fully Operational After March Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Telehealth Giant Him & Hers Announces Data Breach
item.description:
The direct-to-consumer telehealth company Him & Hers has experienced a data breach. In early February, an unauthorized third party gained […] The post Telehealth Giant Him & Hers Announces Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.description:
Verification alert Listings attributed to NOVA have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: NOVA | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

Verification alert Listings attributed to SPACEBEARS have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: SPACEBEARS | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: Moscow, Idaho, clinics reopen after Gritman cyber incident
item.description:
DysruptionHub reports: Gritman Medical Center began reopening clinics in Moscow, Idaho, on Friday after a cybersecurity incident disrupted outpatient care beginning early Wednesday, though the hospital and emergency department remained open throughout. Gritman first publicly disclosed the problem Thursday, saying several primary and specialty clinics were closed because of an electronic systems outage. In an update later that night,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Fitness tracking under scrutiny as Strava military data leak exposes personnel
item.description:
Paulo Vargas reports: Your Strava runs might feel private, but a new Strava military data leak shows how easily that information can reveal more than your workout. In the latest case, activity logs have been linked to more than 500 UK military personnel, connecting everyday exercise to sensitive locations. This goes beyond visible routes. Shared histories and account details... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Researchers didn’t want to glamorize cybercrims. So they roasted them.
item.description:
Jessica Lyons reports: Cybercrime crews have become almost mystical entities, with security vendors assigning them names like Wizard Spider and Velvet Tempest. They hide out in hidden corners of the dark web (often accompanied by a clearnet leak site), leading some infosec folks to talk about these miscreants as if they are invincible. But not... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
item.description:
Brian Krebs reports: An elusive hacker who went by the handle “UNKN” and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Two data security incidents affected immigration law firms and their clients
item.description:
DocketWise is an immigration and case management solution designed for immigration attorneys. The firm informed the Maine Attorney General’s Office on April 3 of a September 1, 2025, data breach that affected the personal information of its law firm clients’ clients. The types of information involved varied by individual client. Their notification to Maine states... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: NYS school data incidents rose 72% in 2025, with 44 reported on Long Island
item.description:
Lorena Mongelli reports: Reports of compromised student data and cybersecurity in schools surged statewide in 2025, according to education officials. Statewide, data incident reports rose 72%, from 384 in 2024 to 662 in 2025, an annual report issued by the state Education Department’s chief privacy officer found. On Long Island, schools reported 44 data incidents in 2025, a jump from 35 the year prior, according to... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Two Breaches, One Quarter: Valley Family Health Care’s Challenging Start to 2026
item.description:
On January 12, Valley Family Health Care (VFHC) notified HHS after learning that the TriZetto Provider Solutions (TPS) breach had affected 4,300 of their patients. The TPS breach, which began in November 2024, involved their patients’ names, addresses, dates of birth, Social Security numbers, health insurance member numbers (including Medicare beneficiary identifiers), health insurer names,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Microsoft links Medusa ransomware affiliate to zero-day attacks
item.description:
Sergiu Gatlan reports: Microsoft says that Storm-1175, a China-based financially motivated cybercriminal group known for deploying Medusa ransomware payloads, has been deploying n-day and zero-day exploits in high-velocity attacks. This cybercrime gang quickly shifts to targeting new security vulnerabilities to gain access to its victims’ networks, weaponizing some of them within a day and, in... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Maine House advances McCabe bill to strengthen cybersecurity at Maine hospitals
item.description:
A press release on April 6, 2026 from Maine House Democrats: On Thursday, the Maine House voted unanimously to advance a bill from Rep. Julie McCabe, D-Lewiston, that would help prevent cybersecurity attacks on Maine hospitals and ensure continuity of patient care when future cyberattacks occur. As amended, LD 2103 would require Maine hospitals to adopt a... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Jones Day confirms limited breach after phishing attack by Silent Ransom Group
item.description:
One of the top-ranked law firms in the country confirmed today that it has suffered a data breach. Jones Day disclosed the breach after hackers known as Silent Ransom Group (SRG) posted the data ​to their dark web leak site on March 30. A spokesperson for the firm said that limited files for 10 clients... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: AT Packaging
item.description:
AT Packaging reported a ransomware. Attributed to DragonForce.
🎭 Threat Actor: DragonForce | 📰 Source: BreachSense | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: Unity National Bank of Houston
